data.narrative:
Scout Space is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $18M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-market comparable companies in the same sector, including ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Aalyria, and Sift.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $18M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a $36M valuation. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $400M implied valuation; this peer set (ClearSpace at $400M, Hubble Network at $400M, Sift at $274M, Aalyria at $1.3B) spans a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not applicable due to absence of Scout Space's revenue figures. 

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $31M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $218M across the two applicable methods. Stage-progression anchors the lower bound conservatively; peer-median data supports substantially higher valuations but reflects meaningful dispersion driven by technology differentiation and market timing. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peer benchmarks, and whether Scout Space's technical positioning aligns with higher-valued or lower-valued peer segments.
